video:video.video:title: Norman Rockwell sketches of White House visitors go on display
video:video.video:description: A series of Norman Rockwell sketches from the 1940s depicting White House visitors in the West Wing lobby are going on public display for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Some facts about the strongest earthquakes ever recorded
video:video.video:description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, causing widespread damage, collapsing buildings and sending panicked residents into the streets.

video:video.video:title: Vermont becomes the first state to ban paraquat, a weedkiller widely used in US agriculture
video:video.video:description: Vermont is set to become the first U.S. state to ban paraquat, a commonly used herbicide that some experts say is linked to Parkinson’s disease.

video:video.video:title: Shops suffer as a powerful 7.2 magnitude earthquake strikes off northern Japan
video:video.video:description: A powerful earthquake struck off the northern coast of Japan during rush hour on Thursday morning, but the country’s meteorological agency said there was no danger of a tsunami.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III is expected to reveal his personal tax bill in a bid to boost transparency
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles is expected to reveal his personal tax bill on Thursday, becoming the first British monarch to do so.

video:video.video:title: Fans erupt in celebrations as Mexico beats Czech Republic 3-0 to complete perfect group stage
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Mexican fans celebrated in the rain after their team's victory over the Czech Republic 3-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup round of 32.

video:video.video:title: Police find six dead in Mechanicville, New York apartment
video:video.video:description: Police say four children and two adults were found dead inside an apartment in Mechanicville, New York, while police were conducting a welfare check on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Coastal city north of Caracas suffers heavy damage after earthquake
video:video.video:description: Aerial images on Thursday show the destruction caused by the earthquake that struck Venezuela in the coastal city of Catia La Mar, north of the capital, Caracas.

video:video.video:title: Chaos in Venezuelan airport after massive earthquakes
video:video.video:description: A pair of powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela, tearing down buildings, closing the country’s main airport and sending panicked residents of the capital pouring into the streets.

video:video.video:title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slam Venezuela, collapsing buildings in Caracas
video:video.video:description: Powerful 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes slammed Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas and leaving residents shaken.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a powerful earthquake rocks Venezuela
video:video.video:description: A pair of powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela, tearing down buildings, closing the country’s main airport and sending panicked residents of the capital pouring into the streets.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Vespa enthusiasts celebrate in Rome on 80th anniversary of the iconic Italian scooter
video:video.video:description: The Piaggio company switched gears from making military vehicles to the iconic scooter at the end of World War ll.

video:video.video:title: Global Empowerment Mission sends aid to Venezuela after earthquakes
video:video.video:description: An international disaster relief organization based Florida is mobilizing aid after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela. (AP Video: Cody Jackson)

video:video.video:title: Native Americans commemorate victory at Little Bighorn with horse races, dance and song
video:video.video:description: Native American tribes are marking the 150th anniversary of the Battle of Greasy Grass, where Lt. Col. George Armstrong Custer was defeated and killed.

video:video.video:title: Lizzo: 'Music has saved my life' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Lizzo's latest studio album "B****" may not be climbing pop charts like her last two did. But she says she's still connecting with her fans and forging her own path in the music industry.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Oil tankers use new route through Strait of Hormuz despite Iranian threats
video:video.video:description: Several tankers have made their way out of the Strait of Hormuz using a new route promoted by a U.N. maritime agency.

video:video.video:title: Haitians in Springfield say they fear for future after court lets Trump end legal protections
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court on Thursday allowed the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants fleeing violence and natural disaster in Haiti and Syria.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Norman Rockwell sketches of White House visitors go on display
video:video.video:description: For over 40 years, sketches by American illustrator Norman Rockwell of scenes from the White House visitor’s lobby graced the walls of the West Wing.

video:video.video:title: Seth Rogen not immune to criticism
video:video.video:description: At the Los Angeles premiere for new movie "The Invite," Seth Rogen discusses how he deals with criticism.

video:video.video:title: Why do earthquakes happen?
video:video.video:description: Earthquakes typically occur along edges of tectonic plates. But their impacts may be felt in a broader region. (AP video produced by Javier Arciga)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 25
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday June 25th: Two massive earthquakes in Venezuela; Senate Republicans reject war powers resolution; Trump holds campaign-style rally to kick off America’s 250 celebration; Ecuadorian and German World Cup fans pack New York’s Times Square.

video:video.video:title: Germany and Ecuador World Cup fans fill New York’s Times Square with flags, drums, music and noise
video:video.video:description: Fans of the Germany and Ecuador World Cup teams filled New York City’s Times Square on Wednesday, ahead of Thursday’s World Cup match.

video:video.video:title: Prime Minister Takaichi says earthquake has struck northern Japan
video:video.video:description: Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi urged residents to remain cautious after a powerful earthquake struck off the eastern coast of Iwate in northeastern Japan on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Prime Minister Takaichi says earthquake struck northern Japan
video:video.video:description: A powerful earthquake struck off Japan’s northern coast on Thursday, but the country’s meteorological agency said there was no danger of a tsunami.

video:video.video:title: The true inspiration behind 'Jackass' stunts
video:video.video:description: As Johnny Knoxville launches "Jackass: Best and Last," he reveals the inspiration behind the series' infamous pranks and stunts.

video:video.video:title: International rescue teams and aid arrive in Venezuela to help earthquake response
video:video.video:description: Rescue teams have begun arriving in the hard-hit Venezuelan city of La Guaira to assist with search and rescue operations after a pair of powerful earthquakes.

video:video.video:title: Iconic mountain rescue dogs keep history alive as museum celebrates first birthday
video:video.video:description: At the millennia-old Great St Bernard Pass, the famous dogs still walk the same mountain paths their ancestors patrolled for more than three centuries.

video:video.video:title: National pride meets breathable mesh: A look at the design of World Cup uniforms
video:video.video:description: Heat, moisture and national pride - a Nike executive talks about what guided the company when making the 2026 World Cup shirts and uniforms. (AP Video by Claire Rush)

video:video.video:title: Europe's extreme heat would be impossible without climate change, scientists say
video:video.video:description: The record-breaking heat that's scorching Europe day and night this month would not have been possible without climate change, according to a new study.

video:video.video:title: Greece becomes first country to use dedicated satellite network to detect wildfires
video:video.video:description: As Greece braces for another blistering wildfire season, a new line of defense is taking shape in Earth’s orbit.

video:video.video:title: Residents in La Guaira search for survivors in rubble after Venezuela earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Residents of La Guaira, north of Venezuela's capital, Caracas, searched on their own Thursday for people trapped after an earthquake struck the country and caused buildings to collapse.

video:video.video:title: Dutch fans flood Kansas City with orange as the ‘Oranjebus’ makes its way downtown
video:video.video:description: The Oranjebus made its way through downtown Kansas City toward the FIFA Fan Festival ahead of a World Cup match between the Netherlands and Tunisia.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela
video:video.video:description: A pair of powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela, tearing down buildings, closing the country’s main airport and sending panicked residents of the capital pouring into the streets.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after completion of costly refurbishment
video:video.video:description: King Charles will not live at Buckingham Palace after the completion of a 10-year, 369 million-pound refurbishment program as the monarchy seeks to increase public access to the historic building that has been the center of royal life for almost 200 years.

video:video.video:title: Millie Bobby Brown welcomes husband to 'Enola Holmes' team
video:video.video:description: Millie Bobby Brown explains what husband Jake Bongiovi brought as an executive producer on new action sequel, "Enola Holmes 3."

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans go crazy for ‘Canadian clapper’ maple leaf-shaped noisemakers given out in Toronto
video:video.video:description: A little red noisemaker shaped like a maple leaf has become all the rage in Toronto during the World Cup. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 26
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, June 26th: Crews in Venezuela search for earthquake survivors; Supreme Court ruling exposes hundreds of thousands to potential deportation; UN pauses the evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Burmese authorities torch $600 million in seized heroin, meth and other drugs
video:video.video:description: Thick clouds of black smoke billowed into the sky on the outskirts of Myanmar's largest city Friday as authorities burned more than 50 tons of heroin, opium, ketamine, methamphetamine, marijuana and crystal meth — some $600 million of confiscated illegal drugs destroyed nationwide.

video:video.video:title: Swedish climate minister shows up to EU meeting with her baby
video:video.video:description: Sweden’s minister for climate and the environment, Romina Pourmokhtari attended a European Council Environment meeting in Luxembourg with her three-month-old baby on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Worldwide celebrations on Global Beatles Day
video:video.video:description: After fan Faith Cohen started Global Beatles Day in 2009, the event went official this year, with support from Apple Corps Ltd and even more Fab Four-themed celebrations across the globe.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Shiite Muslims commemorate Ashoura in the Iraqi holy city of Karbala
video:video.video:description: Shiite Muslims across the world marked Ashoura on Thursday, a holy day symbolizing sacrifice and martyrdom.

video:video.video:title: Cody Johnson: 'The best is yet to come'
video:video.video:description: Country music star Cody Johnson looks forward to release of new album "Banks of the Trinity," says it signals where he's headed as an artist.

video:video.video:title: Vance says Watergate would be a '12-hour news story' today
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance said the Watergate scandal that brought down President Richard Nixon would have been a 12-hour blip in today's news cycle, and he drew parallels between Nixon and President Donald Trump — arguing both were targeted by “deep state” forces.

video:video.video:title: A historic heat wave catches Europe's fashion industry unprepared
video:video.video:description: Paris Fashion Week Men’s became a test of fashion’s relationship with heat, pushing houses to move shows earlier, hand out ice packs and build runways around waves, fog and mist.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after completion of costly refurbishment
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after the completion of a 10-year, 369 million-pound ($487 million) refurbishment program as the monarchy seeks to increase public access to the historic building that has been the center of royal life for almost 200 years.

video:video.video:title: Tokyo celebrates as Japan secures World Cup knockout spot
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans in Tokyo celebrated on Friday after Japan drew with Sweden in their final World Cup group stage match, sending both sides through to the knockout round.

video:video.video:title: The scars of war among survivors in Sudan
video:video.video:description: Three years of war have devastated much of Sudan. The impact has been pressed into the skin of survivors, and their memories. Here are some of their stories.

video:video.video:title: Fear of cartel violence reshapes World Cup celebrations across parts of Mexico
video:video.video:description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country's three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11.

video:video.video:title: Louis Partridge: From 'Enola Holmes' to 007?
video:video.video:description: Actor Louis Partridge reacts to talk he'll be the next James Bond, as he returns for "Enola Holmes 3."

video:video.video:title: Fight erupts in Georgian Parliament amid deepening political tensions
video:video.video:description: A fistfight erupted in the Georgian Parliament on Friday during Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze's annual address.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelan mother desperately looks for missing son as residents wait for aid after earthquake
video:video.video:description: Residents of La Guaira, one of the areas hardest hit by Wednesday's earthquake in Venezuela surveyed the destruction Thursday waiting for official aid to arrive.

video:video.video:title: Ex-national security adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to illegally retaining classified information
video:video.video:description: Bolton's plea agreement with the Justice Department may enable him to avoid time behind bars, but the judge ultimately will decide his punishment.

video:video.video:title: Animals at Rome zoo get cool refreshments to help them cope with high temperatures
video:video.video:description: Animals at a zoo in Rome were given frozen treats to help them cool off as the Italian capital sizzled in a scorching heatwave on Friday.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains Supreme Court allowing Trump administration to end protections for Haitians and Syrians
video:video.video:description: The 6-3 decision overturns lower court orders and allows the Department of Homeland Security to swiftly end temporary protected status, a program that protects a total of 1.3 million people from 17 countries.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Bodies of siblings pulled from rubble of apartment as Venezuela earthquake rescue efforts continue
video:video.video:description: Families in the Venezuelan city of La Guaira mourned loved ones and searched for survivors Thursday after a pair of powerful earthquakes brought down apartment buildings and left scores of people trapped beneath the rubble across the country.

video:video.video:title: Small aircraft crashes into Beijing’s tallest building, according to flight tracking service
video:video.video:description: A small aircraft crashed into Beijing’s tallest building on Friday, the global flight tracking service provider Flightradar24 confirmed, following witness accounts and evacuations in the city's business district.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement with US in 'first step' toward peace, Rubio says
video:video.video:description: The officials did not share details on the agreement that was signed by Yechiel Leiter, the Israeli ambassador to the U.S., and Nada Hamadeh, ambassador of Lebanon to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Newsom urges a national 'billionaires' tax' while fighting one in California
video:video.video:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom is calling for a national “billionaires' tax” and suggests the U.S. government should own a stake in AI companies.

video:video.video:title: Desperate search in Venezuela as anxious families wait to see if relatives survived earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Desperate search in Venezuela as anxious families wait to see if relatives survived earthquakes

video:video.video:title: Trump says ‘nobody saw’ drone that hit cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Describing the U.S. response to a drone strike that President Donald Trump blamed on Iran, the president said one got through unnoticed and struck a ship on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelans pull loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Venezuelans took the search for missing loved ones into their own hands Friday in the aftermath of back-to-back earthquakes, citing the scarcity of government rescuers, as the human toll of the disaster climbed to at least 920 dead and more than 3,300 hurt.

video:video.video:title: Designing spacesuits for future Artemis moon landings
video:video.video:description: Artemis IV astronauts are getting a new spacesuit for their anticipated moon landing. Axiom Space and Prada have teamed up for the suit's design elements and seamstresses in Houston are laying down stitches for this and future space missions.

video:video.video:title: Parts of Mexico with cartel violence celebrate World Cup differently
video:video.video:description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country’s three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11.

video:video.video:title: Norway’s Fans Ready For Battle At Boston Stadium
video:video.video:description: Norway fans are hyped up before its game against France as they take over Patriot Place before heading into Boston Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts.

video:video.video:title: Touring Trump's Washington: How the president is putting his imprint on the nation's capital
video:video.video:description: The United States is celebrating its 250th year. And what better way to mark that anniversary than with an American summer staple — a trip to the nation's capital.

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'You'll find out' if Iran will face consequences for ceasefire violation
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump declined to say whether Iran will face consequences for allegedly violating its ceasefire with the U.S., saying Friday, "You'll find out," after accusing Tehran of carrying out a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Election worker says ICE officers confronted her at polling place over social media post
video:video.video:description: A poll worker said two federal officers visited her polling place during New York's party primaries to confront her about a social media post criticizing the ICE officer who fatally shot Renee Good in Minneapolis.

video:video.video:title: Police officer rescues woman whose vehicle fell into sinkhole in Virginia
video:video.video:description: A woman whose vehicle fell into a water-filled sinkhole has a Norfolk police officer and several others to thank for coming to her rescue.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 26th: US conducts strikes against Iran; Bolton pleads guilty in classified information case; Venezuelan rescue effort after deadly quakes; Ukrainian soldiers held by Russia return home.

video:video.video:title: 10 of 12 jurors say Palisades Fire suspect isn’t guilty. Now he faces a retrial
video:video.video:description: The man accused of sparking last year’s deadly Palisades Fire will be tried again this fall after his first federal arson case ended in a mistrial Friday.

video:video.video:title: French fans celebrate while Norwegians shrug off defeat at World Cup as both sides progress
video:video.video:description: Jubilant French fans expressed their joy after watching their side beat Norway 4-1 on Friday to win their World Cup group.

video:video.video:title: Cody Johnson pays tribute to 'hero' Alan Jackson
video:video.video:description: "You can't say country music is without saying Alan Jackson," says Cody Johnson on the eve of the country superstar's farewell concert to be held Saturday in Nashville.

video:video.video:title: Drone video shows search through rubble of collapsed buildings in La Guaira after Venezuela quakes
video:video.video:description: Footage showed the devastation caused to homes and buildings in the Venezuelan coastal city of La Guaira after a rare double earthquake ravaged the country on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Royal herd of cattle in Rwanda treated to poetry and cultural reverence
video:video.video:description: Rwanda’s renowned Inyambo cows graze at the King’s Palace Museum, symbolizing the nation’s rich cultural heritage.

video:video.video:title: Argentina in Texas raises rivalry among beef lovers at World Cup
video:video.video:description: Drop thousands of Argentina fans into Texas for the World Cup and the debate is inevitable: who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat?

video:video.video:title: Son of a murdered voting rights activist recalls his family's sacrifice as Voting Rights Act weakens
video:video.video:description: Dennis Dahmer Sr., whose voting rights activist father was killed by the Ku Klux Klan, wants to remind people the price his family and other families have paid for Black people to have the right to vote.

video:video.video:title: CENTCOM releases video of US strike on Iran after attack on cargo ship
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command has released video purporting to show one of its strikes on Iran launched in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelans take search for the missing into their own hands as earthquake death toll climbs
video:video.video:description: Omar Guariato was celebrating his granddaughter's 5th birthday at home when the earthquakes began. As he sat in a camp for displaced people in the hard-hit La Guaira, he recalled the moment the house next door collapsed.
